idea.rationale: Add a Controllability-Guided Latent Conditioner (CGLC): a small neural module that computes a minimal-control-energy score from an approximate nonlinear controllability Gramian and injects it into the conditional flow-matching network as a scalar conditioning feature and attention bias during trajectory generation. The CGLC is trained jointly with the flow-matching loss using an explicit minimum-control-energy regularizer (L_control) that penalizes sampled trajectories requiring high control energy, weighted against the value-guidance term.

delta.intervention: Add a Controllability-Guided Latent Conditioner (CGLC): a small neural module that computes a minimal-control-energy score from an approximate nonlinear controllability Gramian and injects it into the conditional flow-matching network as a scalar conditioning feature and attention bias during trajectory generation. The CGLC is trained jointly with the flow-matching loss using an explicit minimum-control-energy regularizer (L_control) that penalizes sampled trajectories requiring high control energy, weighted against the value-guidance term.
delta.implementation_notes: CGLC architecture: MLP( state_embedding -> 32 -> 16 -> scalar ) producing log-control-energy. Gramian approximation: use time-discretized empirical controllability Gramian over short windows (length k, e.g., k=8–16 steps) computed from model-free rollouts in a learned latent-action linearization (small linearization head). Compute Gramian via low-rank randomized SVD on the k-step empirical controllability matrix to produce approximate minimal control-energy cheaply. Inject the scalar (normalized) into the flow-matching model by concatenation with existing initial-state conditioning and as a soft additive attention bias at each denoising timestep. Training losses: existing conditional flow-matching loss + lambda_ctrl * L_control + lambda_val * L_value (value guidance retained). Budget impact: modest — extra MLP (~3k params) and periodic low-rank SVD on small matrices (k<=16, rank r<=4). Additional compute is ~+5–10% per training update; memory negligible. If strict budget_ceiling exists, reduce k or SVD frequency (e.g., compute Gramian every N_updates).

regression_tests: Reproduce parent key metrics: sample-efficiency curves (episodic return vs environment steps) on at least two benchmark tasks (one short-horizon like Cartpole-like, one long-horizon like Humanoid or long-horizon locomotion) under the same online interaction-limited budget; report returns and variance over 5 seeds.
Measure model-error compounding proxy: compare predicted vs actual cumulative reward for generated trajectories of increasing length (trajectory mismatch) and show no degradation relative to parent.
ablation_tests: CGLC ablation: same model but without injecting CGLC scalar (ablated conditioner) to measure its effect on returns and control-energy statistics.
Gramian approximation ablation: replace low-rank SVD Gramian with a trivial identity proxy to isolate Gramian approximation value.
Loss ablation: set lambda_ctrl=0 to test effect of the control-energy regularizer while keeping CGLC architecture.
stress_tests: Controllability stress: evaluate under tasks with intentionally low controllability (e.g., added actuator noise, partial actuator failure) and measure whether CGLC leads to higher robustness in sampled trajectories than parent.

Distribution shift stress: perturb initial-state conditioning distribution (shift mean, expand variance) to measure whether CGLC induces over-conservative trajectory sampling or fails under unseen initial states.
Compute-limited stress: run with reduced Gramian frequency and smaller k to quantify degradation and validate the performance vs compute tradeoff.

risk_patch: Over-conservatism: CGLC may bias sampling toward low-energy (but lower-return) trajectories; mitigate by tuning lambda_ctrl and maintaining value-guidance term; include ablation that scans lambda_ctrl.

Approximation error: low-rank Gramian may misestimate controllability in highly nonlinear regimes, causing poor conditioning; mitigate by stress-test and fallback to identity proxy when approximation confidence (e.g., singular value gap) is low.
Compute overhead: SVD and extra conditioning increase compute; mitigate by frequency throttling and small k/r choices and monitor training throughput telemetry.

idea.abstract: Replace step-wise dynamics modeling in online model-based RL with a conditional flow-matching trajectory generator that directly samples fixed-length state-action trajectories from initial states. Augment the generator with a Controllability-Guided Latent Conditioner (CGLC) which maps a low-rank empirical nonlinear controllability Gramian (top-k singular-spectrum and optional low-rank projections) to a normalized log-minimum-control-energy (MCE) scalar and a small attention-bias vector. CGLC: small MLP (2–3 layers, hidden 32–128) that inputs top-k singular values of an empirical time-discretized nonlinear controllability Gramian (k=8–32 via randomized low-rank SVD) plus optional low-rank energy projection coefficients and outputs: (i) a normalized log-MCE scalar to concatenate to the initial-state conditioning vector and (ii) an attention-bias vector matched to the flow model's attention head dimension (or small FiLM scales for non-attention flows). Benchmarks: at least one short-horizon control (e.g., Cartpole-like/inverted pendulum) and one long-horizon locomotion/control (e.g., Humanoid or comparable long-horizon task) to probe horizon-dependent effects. idea.risks: Over-conservatism: CGLC + L_control may bias sampling to low-energy, lower-return trajectories. Mitigation: tune λ_ctrl_base vs λ_val, use ATCG to reduce λ_ctrl_eff when confidence is low, perform λ_ctrl sweeps and include ablations (λ_ctrl=0). idea.rationale: Add an Adaptive-Temperature Confidence Gate (ATCG) that modulates (a) the control-energy regularizer weight λ_ctrl and (b) the CGLC attention-bias scale as a smooth function of the Gramian-confidence metric. ATCG outputs a scalar α ∈ [0,1] computed from the Gramian-confidence (sv_gap) via a small monotonic neural calibration curve (2-layer MLP with sigmoid), producing effective λ_ctrl_eff = α · λ_ctrl_base and attention_bias_eff = α · attention_bias_base. When confidence is very low α→0 (falls back to identity-proxy behavior and disables strong control regularization); when confidence is high...

delta.intervention: Add an Adaptive-Temperature Confidence Gate (ATCG) that modulates (a) the control-energy regularizer weight λ_ctrl and (b) the CGLC attention-bias scale as a smooth function of the Gramian-confidence metric. ATCG outputs a scalar α ∈ [0,1] computed from the Gramian-confidence (sv_gap) via a small monotonic neural calibration curve (2-layer MLP with sigmoid), producing effective λ_ctrl_eff = α · λ_ctrl_base and attention_bias_eff = α · attention_bias_base. When confidence is very low α→0 (falls back to identity-proxy behavior and disables strong control regularization); when confidence is high α→1 full conditioning is used. The ATCG parameters are trained jointly with CGLC but constrained by an L2 prior to prevent runaway scaling.
delta.implementation_notes: Compute Gramian-confidence sv_gap at the same frequency F used by parent. Pass sv_gap through the 2-layer calibration MLP (hidden 16 units, ReLU, sigmoid output) to produce α. Multiply existing CGLC scalar conditioning and attention-bias vectors by α; set λ_ctrl_eff = α·λ_ctrl. Add L2 regularizer (weight 1e-3) on MLP params. Budget impact: negligible at inference (one tiny MLP per update), small extra compute when evaluating sv_gap—no extra SVDs. If compute-limited, reduce F or reuse last α for up to T_steps. Annotate instrumentation to log α, sv_gap, λ_ctrl_eff per training batch for guardrails.

regression_tests: Reproduce parent key metrics: sample-efficiency curves (episodic return vs environment steps) on at least two benchmark tasks (one short-horizon like Cartpole-like, one long-horizon like Humanoid or long-horizon locomotion) under identical online interaction-limited budgets; report mean and std over 5 seeds.
Measure model-error compounding proxy: compare predicted vs actual cumulative reward for generated trajectories of increasing length and show no degradation relative to parent (trajectory mismatch).
ablation_tests: ATCG ablation: full model but replace ATCG with fixed α=1 (i.e., original CGLC behavior) to measure whether adaptivity improves returns and safety.
Zero-confidence ablation: force α=0 (always fallback to identity proxy) to isolate benefit of Gramian signal vs identity.
λ_ctrl ablation: set λ_ctrl_base=0 while keeping ATCG active to test effect of adaptive gating without explicit control-energy loss.
Calibration-head ablation: replace 2-layer MLP with a linear mapping from sv_gap to α to test whether nonlinear calibration matters.
stress_tests: Controllability stress: evaluate on tasks with degraded actuators (added actuator noise, partial actuator failure) and measure robustness of sampled trajectories and returns compared to parent and ATCG-ablations.

Distribution shift stress: perturb initial-state conditioning distribution (shift mean, expand variance) to measure whether ATCG prevents over-conservative behavior and preserves returns under unseen initial states.
Compute-limited stress: run with reduced Gramian frequency F×10 and smaller k to quantify performance vs compute tradeoff; measure stability of α when sv_gap is stale.

risk_patch: ATCG may underweight λ_ctrl when Gramian-confidence is mildly miscalibrated, causing temporary loss of robustness — mitigation: log α and sv_gap, include the zero-confidence ablation, and choose α prior bias toward safety (e.g., clip minimal α_min>0 if required).
Additional complexity may mask failure modes — mitigation: expose α trajectories in dashboards and include deliberate failure-mode tests (actuator degradation) in regression suite.

anchor_mapping.M4: Preserve: The design continues to replace step-wise dynamics with direct trajectory generation; ATCG affects only conditioning weightings and regularization, not switching back to step-wise models (M4 preserved).
anchor_mapping.E1: Preserve: Regression tests keep the online MBRL protocol with interaction-limited budgets; ATCG adds no extra environment interactions (E1 preserved).

M4.reason: ATCG does not reintroduce step-wise predictions; it refines how CGLC conditions the trajectory generator, so the replacement mechanism remains.

Adversarial/task-specific dynamics where high return requires temporarily high control energy; MCE prior + gating biases away from necessary maneuvers (over-co...
Reduced Gramian update frequency (compute-limited setting) makes α stale, leading to mis-gating and performance regressions under distribution shift
evaluation.fairness_protocol: Mostly adequate: you specify (i) same online interaction budgets, (ii) same generator as parent without CGLC/ATCG, (iii) CGLC with fixed α=1, plus step-wise MBRL baselines, and multi-seed reporting. Missing/underspecified: exact data-collection policy parity (how trajectories are executed/replanned), equal wall-clock or equal model-update counts (important since Gramian/SVD cost varies), and hyperparameter budget fairness (λ_ctrl/λ_val/ATCG tuning should be matched across ablations/baselines). Also add a 'matched compute' condition where baselines get extra updates to offset Gramian overhead.
evaluation.feedback: This operator is a classic 'add a gate' incremental tweak (mechanism-commit-innovation on top of CGLC) and does not add a new scientific mechanism beyond confidence-weighting; ICML bar risk: novelty is low unless you can (a) justify sv_gap→calibrated α as a principled uncertainty estimator for controllability regularization, and (b) demonstrate new failure science (when/why Gramian misestimation hurts, and how gating changes that). To strengthen: (1) define sv_gap precisely and validate it as a predictor of Gramian estimation error (correlate sv_gap with held-out controllability/MCE estimation error or with downstream return deltas); (2) compare against simpler alternatives (hard threshold, EMA smoothing of λ_ctrl, or Bayesian/ensemble Gramian confidence) to show the calibration head is n...
evaluation.defect_fix_summary: Addresses defects ['Gramian misestimation', 'sensitivity to Gramian-confidence thresholding', partially 'over-conservatism'] by replacing a brittle fixed-strength controllability prior with a smooth, learned confidence gate that downweights λ_ctrl and attention bias when Gramian confidence is low, reducing harmful misconditioning from noisy low-rank Gramian estimates.

stress_tests: Controllability stress: evaluate under tasks with intentionally low controllability (e.g., added actuator noise, partial actuator failure) and measure whether CGLC leads to higher robustness in sampled trajectories than parent.

Distribution shift stress: perturb initial-state conditioning distribution (shift mean, expand variance) to measure whether CGLC induces over-conservative trajectory sampling or fails under unseen initial states.
Compute-limited stress: run with reduced Gramian frequency and smaller k to quantify degradation and validate the performance vs compute tradeoff.

risk_patch: Over-conservatism: CGLC may bias sampling toward low-energy (but lower-return) trajectories; mitigate by tuning lambda_ctrl and maintaining value-guidance term; include ablation that scans lambda_ctrl.

Approximation error: low-rank Gramian may misestimate controllability in highly nonlinear regimes, causing poor conditioning; mitigate by stress-test and fallback to identity proxy when approximation confidence (e.g., singular value gap) is low.
Compute overhead: SVD and extra conditioning increase compute; mitigate by frequency throttling and small k/r choices and monitor training throughput telemetry.

anchor_mapping.M1: Preserve — CGLC injects controllability scalar into the existing conditional flow-matching generator, leaving trajectory-conditioned architecture intact; experiment: ablation removing conditioner to show M1 functionality preserved.
anchor_mapping.M2: Addressed — the intervention explicitly computes a minimum-control-energy surrogate via the low-rank_empirical_Gramian and trains the CGLC with L_control to operationalize the controllability objective.
anchor_mapping.M3: Preserve/Enhance — CGLC augments (not replaces) the existing value-guidance term by biasing the generator away from high-control-energy trajectories, with combined loss balancing value and control.
anchor_mapping.M4: Preserve — the approach retains replacement of step-wise dynamics with direct trajectory generation; CGLC is a conditioner only and does not introduce step-wise predictors.
anchor_mapping.E1: Preserve — experiments keep the online MBRL protocol and interaction-limited budgets; regression tests re-run sample-efficiency under identical budgets.
anchor_mapping.E2: Preserve — measure episodic return and sample-efficiency; include explicit trajectory mismatch diagnostics to assess long-horizon degradation.
anchor_mapping.E3: Preserve — comparisons include standard step-wise model-based RL baselines under identical online training conditions as regression tests require.

M1.reason: CGLC only conditions the flow-matching generator; it does not alter the fact that full trajectories are produced conditioned on initial states.

M2.reason: The intervention explicitly implements a minimum-control-energy surrogate via an empirical Gramian and uses it as a training regularizer.

M3.reason: Value guidance remains in the loss; CGLC provides complementary bias toward controllable, potentially higher-fidelity trajectories.

M4.reason: No step-wise dynamics models are introduced; conditioning is applied to trajectory-level generator.

E1.reason: Experimental protocol remains online MBRL with interaction limits; added instrumentation does not change data budget.

E2.reason: New diagnostics are added but the core metrics (episodic return, returns vs env steps) are preserved and measured.

E3.reason: Baselines and training conditions are unchanged; CGLC is tested in head-to-head comparisons.

evaluation.fairness_protocol: Good start: same online interaction budget (E1) and explicit baselines (E3) are stated, plus ablations (remove CGLC, lambda_ctrl=0, identity-Gramian). Missing controls: (1) normalize by wall-clock or number of gradient updates since added SVD increases compute; (2) compare against a similarly-parameterized conditioner that uses simple proxies (e.g., ||a||^2 or learned energy predictor without Gramian) to show the Gramian-specific benefit; (3) ensure identical trajectory length T, sampling steps, and planner call frequency at evaluation time across methods; (4) include at least one stochastic/partial-observation game-like benchmark (not only MuJoCo) to match the stated 'games' motivation.
evaluation.feedback: Mechanism-commit-innovation is substantive (CGLC + low-rank empirical Gramian) and does address the stated defect 'unclear_controllability_integration', but the ICML bar will hinge on (a) demonstrating that controllability guidance improves long-horizon coherence beyond what generic action-penalties/energy-regularization already give, and (b) showing robustness in game-like stochastic/adversarial settings rather than only standard control. Tighten the method by specifying exactly what L_control is (e.g., u^T W^{-1} u surrogate; how u is defined for a trajectory sample), how the linearization head is trained/updated online, and how confidence-based fallback is triggered (singular value gap threshold). evaluation.defect_fix_summary: Addresses defects ['long-horizon_model_error_compounding', 'unclear_controllability_integration', 'validation_gap'] by operationalizing controllability (M2) via a low-rank empirical Gramian and injecting it through CGLC into the flow-matching generator, plus adding targeted ablations/stress-tests to validate whether controllability guidance (not just extra conditioning) improves long-horizon robustness under online MBRL budgets.

idea_contract.scope_statement: Replace step-wise dynamics modeling in online model-based RL with a conditional flow-matching trajectory generator that directly samples high-return trajectories from initial states. Use a minimum-control-energy-based controllability objective (via a nonlinear controllability Gramian) together with value guidance to avoid long-horizon model-error compounding and improve sample efficiency in online MBRL.
idea_contract.thesis: Controllability-guided flow matching for online MBRL
core_claims: A conditional flow-matching trajectory generator can replace step-wise dynamics models in online MBRL by directly sampling high-return trajectories conditioned on initial states.

Combining a minimum-control-energy controllability objective (computed with a nonlinear controllability Gramian) with value-based guidance reduces long-horizon model-error compounding and improves sample efficiency compared to step-wise model-based baselines.
mechanism_invariants: Conditional flow-matching trajectory generator that produces full trajectories conditioned on initial states
Controllability objective based on minimum control-energy computed via a nonlinear controllability Gramian
Value guidance term that biases trajectory sampling toward high-return trajectories
Design replaces step-wise dynamics prediction with direct trajectory generation during online interaction
evaluation_invariants: Online MBRL protocol with interaction-limited data budgets (compare sample efficiency under the same budget)
Measure episodic return and sample-efficiency (returns vs environment steps) and assess long-horizon degradation/model-error compounding
Compare against standard step-wise model-based RL baselines under identical online training conditions
non_goals: Developing or advocating step-wise dynamics prediction as part of the proposed method
Claims or proofs of global optimality or convergence beyond empirical performance
Targeting offline RL or batch-only learning scenarios
Integrating unrelated model-free algorithmic innovations as part of the core proposal
allowed_axes: architecture and capacity of the flow-matching model (e.g., network size, parameterization)
approximation methods for the nonlinear controllability Gramian
form and strength of the value-guidance term (e.g., weighting, learned vs. bootstrapped value)
task/environment selection and horizon lengths within online MBRL benchmarks
initial-state conditioning distributions and trajectory length sampled
